rationale: C_EC_521 CreateRow supplied only non-target columns 8-13, so columns 3-7 were omitted. The C_EC_521 values section says p/r/b/x/y are defaults for omitted columns on new rows, and the Get returned those documented values with SUCCESS.

C_EC_521 column 3 (p) was omitted at CreateRow, so the TPer was required to use the documented P-521 p default. The Get returned 0 for column 3 instead of the default p value, although the other returned columns match their defaults.

Although the returned K-163 default values match the values section, CreateRow explicitly supplied values for columns 8, 9, and 10. The CreateRow rule says successful creation uses the values passed as parameters, so the later Get should not replace those supplied cells with defaults.
concern: The C_EC_163 table-description snippet and values-section naming appear inconsistent around the middle curve columns, but both cited CreateRow semantics and the trajectory's explicit supplied target columns still make the response noncompliant.

rationale: C_EC_163 omitted column 3 should default to K-163 k1 = 07, but the Get returned 0. In addition, columns 8, 9, and 10 were explicitly supplied in CreateRow and should have retained the supplied values rather than default values.
concern: The C_EC_163 table-description snippet and values-section naming appear inconsistent around the middle curve columns; this does not affect the column 3 default failure or the supplied-column conflict.

rationale: For C_EC_233, CreateRow explicitly supplied column 8, which the table description identifies as y. Successful CreateRow uses supplied parameter values, so the Get of column 8 should return the supplied value, not the documented default y value.

C_EC_233 column 3 (k) was omitted and should default to 4A, but the Get returned 0. Also, column 8 was explicitly supplied in CreateRow, so returning the default y for that supplied column conflicts with CreateRow semantics.

For C_EC_283, CreateRow explicitly supplied columns 8, 9, and 10, which the table description identifies as b, x, and y. A successful CreateRow must use supplied parameter values, so the later Get should not return defaults for those supplied columns.

C_EC_283 column 3 (k1) was omitted and should default to 0C, but the Get returned 0. Separately, columns 8, 9, and 10 were supplied in CreateRow and should retain supplied values rather than the default b/x/y values.
